purpose: This commit fixes a bug in the Bluetooth stack by preventing queuing commands when HCI_UNREGISTER is set.
The commit removes an unnecessary include directive in a source file.
The commit updates documentation for a new metric 'N/A' added to store operations.
The commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping.
The commit adds two new supplies (AVDD and MICVDD) to an existing codec binding for a Realtek audio codec.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding an iounmap() call to release a mapped resource.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in the net/mlx5e module.
This commit changes the type of a struct member from unsigned to signed, which is a technical change that does not affect the functionality of the code.
This commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V.
This commit introduces a new function to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
This commit adds a new test for the RTC (Real-Time Clock) driver in the Linux kernel. The test checks if the RTC is present and skips the test if it's not.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to a variable, which cleans up a clang scan build warning.
This commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks in a kernel built with CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y.
The commit removes duplicated text in a header file for KVM's MMU implementation.

This commit reverts a previous change that populated a clock table based on DCFCLK states. The revert is done to prevent an underflow issue caused by how PMFW fills out the table when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU.
The commit renames a struct in the DW PCIe core driver to make it more consistent with other names.
The commit fixes a warning in the clang compiler by using the correct format specifier for an unsigned short value.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS jump_label function by casting the upper bound of the branch range to long to do signed compare, avoiding negative offset triggering a warning.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() that calls a function. This is part of the ongoing effort to get rid of the unwanted hal layer.
The commit declares non-global symbols as static to fix sparse warnings introduced while adding errata.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from display/msm nodes.
This commit is allocating arrays in a struct called ctrl_ref for v4l2 controls.
The commit adds new SoCs and PMICs to the socinfo driver, which is responsible for providing information about the system-on-chip (SoC) and power management integrated circuit (PMIC).
The commit adds recognition of new chip cuts for the rtl8xxxu driver.
The commit drops a custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks in the core.
The commit is a refactoring of the pt_ops macro to move it next to other similar macros, which makes the code more readable.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to support I2C nodes.
The commit fixes a typo in a comment and improves code style by moving a comment to its own line.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when releasing software-based flow control resources.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, which is a technical change that improves the code quality and readability.
The commit removes a hack that was used to check if a file is a swapfile.
The commit adds a new feature to the GTP device driver by implementing an echo response function.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. It also creates a new function called cpumask_first_zero() that was previously missing.
The commit removes a call to an IOVAR that is no longer supported in the firmware, which prevents a failure message from being displayed on the console.
The commit adds support for a new platform without an amplifier by connecting the alc5682 headset codec to SSP0.
This commit adds a new check to fail zerocopy send requests for protocols that don't support it.
The commit adds a test to ensure that xeCJK.sty is loaded only if it exists.
The commit modifies the scripts/checkpatch.pl script to allow macros with complex values enclosed in parentheses.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers for better code sharing among drivers.
The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding workers to ensure parallel execution of three HWs in MT8195.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and replaces ata_scsi_dump_cdb() with scsi tracing.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank, which is a new feature.
The commit fixes a bug in the kernel's Control Flow Integrity (CFI) feature by updating a function pointer prototype to match reality.
The commit renames a property in a device tree binding from 'reset' to 'resets', which is the correct name according to the documentation.
The commit fixes a warning in the GCC compiler by relegating it to W=1+.
The commit uses pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync to simplify code.
The commit splits out fixed file installation and removal from io_uring and moves it to a separate filetable code, which is where it belongs.
The commit splits the G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes in the device tree, allowing for independent power management of each block.
The commit removes a macro that was only used once in the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro.
